page: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. The system provides 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. It is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 5760 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3091.16, placing it in the top 10% of tested instances. Multi-core performance is also strong, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 99520.25, ranking in the top 25%. However, a key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention under sustained multi-tenant workloads. The processor supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX capabilities.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density, combining a substantial 256.0 GB memory capacity with a large 5760 GB local NVMe SSD. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and large memory capacity aligns with workloads such as large-scale databases, memory-cached applications, data warehousing, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring significant local storage.
description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, representing a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio, and includes a bundled 5760 GB NVMe SSD for high-capacity local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance achieved top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3091.16 (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance with a score of 99520.25 (top 25%). A key tradeoff is its shared CPU allocation, which may affect performance consistency. This configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and applications requiring substantial local storage capacity.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 5760 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and 5.7 TB of high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 5760 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark results
Shared CPU allocation model suited for variable workloads
Optimized for large-scale databases and memory-intensive applications
